meaning: adj.团状的,笨重的,不管用的
examples: The carbon loss of coke and the change of coke reactivity index in lumpish section of blast furnace were studied.
The results showed that the carbon loss was increased from top to bottom of the blast furnace lumpish section, but didn't exceed 10%.
The crystal form of the product gained by conventional heating is smooth, while it is lumpish for the product gained by microwave heating.
Wisely, she fled the press hounds, but the only picture of her that photo editors could find to run incessantly made her look rather lumpish.
